entities.name: NANO-BIOFERTILIZERS
entities.description: Biologically synthesized nano-fertilizers from microbes and plants used to promote crop production and protection

entities.name: MICROBES
entities.description: Microorganisms used in the synthesis of nano-biofertilizers

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Plants used in the synthesis of nano-biofertilizers

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops for food or other purposes

entities.name: CROP PROTECTION
entities.description: The practice of managing pests, diseases, and weeds to protect crops

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH-PROMOTING MICROBES
entities.description: Microbes that enhance plant growth and possess antagonistic traits

entities.name: GROWTH PROMOTION
entities.description: The ability of certain microbes to enhance the growth of plants

entities.name: ANTAGONISTIC TRAITS
entities.description: Traits of microbes that inhibit the growth of pathogens

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The upper layer of earth in which plants grow

entities.name: CROPS
entities.description: Cultivated plants grown for food or other purposes

entities.name: MICROBIOME
entities.description: The community of microorganisms in a particular environment

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to function within given limits to sustain plant growth

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that negatively impact plants

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Living organisms that negatively impact plants

entities.name: BIOACTIVE COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Chemicals produced by organisms that have a biological effect

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of crop production per unit area

entities.name: NANOTECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The manipulation of matter on an atomic, molecular, and supramolecular scale

entities.name: NANOPARTICLES
entities.description: Particles with at least one dimension in the range of 1 to 100 nanometers

entities.name: TOXICITY
entities.description: The degree to which a substance can damage an organism

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL FOOTPRINT
entities.description: The impact of human activities on the environment

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The continued capacity of soil to function as a vital living ecosystem
